steps.title: Identify the dominant penalty reason
steps.content: The graph shows tax penalties by the reason they were assessed. Across all three years, undistributed income (the penalty for failing to meet the minimum charitable distribution requirement) clearly dominates, with bars reaching approximately 40%, 75%, and 55% for 2003, 2004, and 2005 respectively. All other reasons have significantly smaller percentages.

steps.title: Match the claim to the data
steps.content: Choice D states that penalties for undistributed income exceeded those for all other reasons combined. The graph confirms this for every year: 2003 shows 40% versus (10%+8%+2%)=20%; 2004 shows 75% versus (40%+15%+2%)=57%; 2005 shows 55% versus (10%+5%+1%)=16%. Choices A and C make weaker single-reason comparisons, and B introduces Form 990 data not shown in the graph. That matches choice D — D is correct.
